title: Deadlocked Wars: How Major Powers Misread the Regions They Attacked
summary: Russia and the United States projected their own centralized views onto Ukraine and Iran, analysts said. As a result, the smaller countries trapped larger ones in a costly confrontation.

title: UK Forces Seize Russian Shadow Fleet Oil Tanker
summary: Britain’s Defense Ministry said it was the first time that British forces had acted alone to stop a ship in the fleet, a collection of vessels that Russia uses to move fuel and evade sanctions.

title: Knicks Give Their City Something New: Impossible Joy
summary: At long last, the team that New York City cares about the most has loved it back.

title: Trump at 80: A President ‘Really Uncomfortable’ With Aging
summary: Even for a president known for imposing his own reality on every situation, Mr. Trump has not outrun scrutiny over his age.

title: Kash Patel Keeps Suing the Press
summary: The F.B.I. director, following a strategy from President Trump, has filed six defamation lawsuits against news media companies and commentators in nearly seven years.

title: Trump Administration Reignites Its Feud With Anthropic Over Latest A.I. Models
summary: The administration’s surprise restrictions Friday cut off foreign access to Anthropic’s latest models and sparked another round of finger pointing.

title: In Age of AI, World’s Leading Deepfake Expert No Longer Trusts His Own Eyes
summary: In the age of A.I., Hany Farid is struggling to prove what’s real before the internet decides for itself.

title: Germany and Japan Are Rearming Again, 80 Years After World War II
summary: After becoming allies to disastrous effect in the 1940s, Berlin and Tokyo are finding new reasons to team up — including rebuilding their militaries.

title: The G7 Summit Is Dogged by Chaos and Divided by Trump
summary: Group of 7 meetings once embodied the effort to sustain the global diplomatic order. This year’s gathering, starting on Monday, symbolizes its fragmentation.

title: The Secrets of a Soccer-Turf Master
summary: John Sorochan, a turf scientist at the University of Tennessee, has led the yearslong, multimillion-dollar effort to develop perfect playing fields for the 2026 World Cup.

title: Slayyyter Had One Last Chance for Pop Stardom, and She Nailed It
summary: After a few attempts that didn’t fully connect, the St. Louis singer was ready to walk away, but not before making a raw album of thrashed pop, “Worst Girl in America.”

title: How Redistricting Pit Wasserman Schultz Against Black Democrats in Florida
summary: Four candidates running in a historically Black district risk dividing the Black vote and losing to Ms. Wasserman Schultz, who is white.

title: President Trump Endorses Mike Collins in Georgia Senate Runoff
summary: Mr. Trump backed Mr. Collins over Derek Dooley, a former football coach supported by Gov. Brian Kemp, a Republican whose relationship with the president is strained.

title: What the Cult of Efficiency Costs Us
summary: The Save Our Bacon Act is bad for pigs and bad for us.

title: Bob Dylan and Liza Minnelli Already Turned 80. They Have Thoughts for Trump.
summary: “I feel the same as I did 50 years ago,” President Trump said. “It’s crazy.”

title: Net Gain
summary: We’re covering the Knicks, and a savvy strategy people are using to game prediction markets.

title: Voters Reject Anti-Islam Candidate in Mayoral Race in Dallas Suburb
summary: The election for mayor of Frisco, Texas, became a referendum on diversity and a test of anti-Muslim messaging in a general election contest.
